title: Replicating He et al. (2023)
code: library(sdim)  # Align dates: he2023_factors ends 12 months earlier than portfolio datasets he2023_ff48 <- he2023_ff48vw[1:516, -1] / 100 - he2023_ff5$RF[127:642] / 100 G <- he2023_factors[1:516, -1] / 100  # First 6 columns are Fama-French 5 + momentum f5 <- G[, 1:6]

title: Replicating He et al. (2023)
code: nfact   <- c(1, 3, 5, 6, 10) methods <- c("FF", "PCA", "PLS", "RRA")  total_r2 <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(methods), ncol = length(nfact)) rownames(total_r2) <- methods colnames(total_r2) <- paste(nfact, "factors")  for (j in seq_along(nfact)) {    k <- nfact[j]    if (k <= 6) {     total_r2["FF", j] <- eval_factors(he2023_ff48, f5[, 1:k])["TotalR2"]   }    fit_pca <- pca_est(target = he2023_ff48, X = G, nfac = k)   total_r2["PCA", j] <- eval_factors(he2023_ff48, fit_pca$factors)["TotalR2"]    fit_pls <- pls_est(target = he2023_ff48, X = G, nfac = k)   total_r2["PLS", j] <- eval_factors(he2023_ff48, fit_pls$factors)["TotalR2"]    fit_rra <- rra_est(target = he2023_ff48, X = G, nfac = k)   total_r2["RRA", j] <- eval_factors(he2023_ff48, fit_rra$factors)["TotalR2"]  }

title: Replicating He et al. (2023)
text: RRA consistently achieves highest total \(R^2\) across factor counts, confirming main finding et al. (2023): reduced-rank approach effectively shrinks factor dimension retaining pricing information.

title: Replicating He et al. (2023)
text: , J., Huang, J., Li, F., Zhou, G. (2023). Shrinking Factor Dimension: Reduced-Rank Approach. Management Science, 69(9). DOI: 10.1287/mnsc.2022.4563

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: package includes authors’ original data: huang2022_macro: 720 × 123 matrix transformed FRED-MD predictors (January 1960 December 2019). huang2022_ip: 720-vector monthly IP growth (log-difference IP index).

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: --sample exercise uses expanding window: Initial estimation window: January 1960 December 1984 (300 months). --sample period: January 1985 December 2019 (420 months). Benchmark: AR model lag order selected SIC (max lag = 1). Factor models: ARDL model AR lags + 1 lag PCA/sPCA factors. sPCA, scaling regression uses predictive relationship \(y_{t+1} ~ X_{,t}\), absolute slopes winsorized 90th percentile. spca_est() function supports directly: length(target) < nrow(X), first length(target) rows used scaling regression rows X standardized used factor extraction.

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: run_oos() function uses select_ar_lag_sic(), estimate_ar_res(), estimate_ardl_multi() AR benchmark ARDL forecasting model, pca_est() / spca_est() predict() factor extraction. loop runs ~420 iterations takes several minutes.
code: run_oos <- function(y, Z, h = 1, p_max = 1, nfac_max = 5) {    TT <- length(y)   M  <- (1984 - 1959) * 12   NN <- TT - M    FC_AR    <- rep(NA, NN - (h - 1))   FC_PCA   <- matrix(NA, NN - (h - 1), nfac_max)   FC_sPCA  <- matrix(NA, NN - (h - 1), nfac_max)   actual_y <- rep(NA, NN - (h - 1))    for (n in seq_len(NN - (h - 1))) {      actual_y[n] <- mean(y[(M + n):(M + n + h - 1)])      y_n  <- y[1:(M + n - 1)]     Z_n  <- Z[1:(M + n - 1), ]     Zs_n <- oos_standardize(Z_n)     T_n  <- length(y_n)      y_n_h <- vapply(       seq_len(T_n - (h - 1)),       function(t) mean(y_n[t:(t + h - 1)]),       numeric(1)     )      # --- AR benchmark with SIC lag selection ---     p_ar <- select_ar_lag_sic(y_n, h, p_max)      if (p_ar > 0L) {        ar_out   <- estimate_ar_res(y_n, h, p_ar)       y_n_last <- rev(y_n[(T_n - p_ar + 1):T_n])       FC_AR[n] <- sum(c(1, y_n_last) * ar_out$a_hat)      } else {        FC_AR[n] <- mean(y_n)      }      # --- PCA factors ---     pca_fit <- pca_est(X = Zs_n, nfac = nfac_max)     z_pc_n  <- predict(pca_fit, Zs_n)      # --- sPCA factors (predictive alignment + winsorization) ---     spca_fit <- spca_est(       target       = y_n_h[2:length(y_n_h)],       X            = Z_n,       nfac         = nfac_max,       winsorize    = TRUE,       winsor_probs = c(0, 90)     )      z_trans_n <- predict(spca_fit, Z_n)      # --- ARDL forecast for each number of factors ---     for (cc in seq_len(nfac_max)) {        for (jj in 1:2) {          z_f <- if (jj == 1) {                      z_pc_n[, 1:cc, drop = FALSE]          } else {                        z_trans_n[, 1:cc, drop = FALSE]                      }          p_ardl <- c(p_ar, 1)          if (p_ar > 0L) {            c_hat    <- estimate_ardl_multi(y_n, z_f, h, p_ardl)           y_n_last <- rev(y_n[(T_n - p_ar + 1):T_n])           fc       <- sum(c(1, y_n_last, z_f[T_n, ]) * c_hat)          } else {            dep   <- y_n_h[2:length(y_n_h)]           reg   <- cbind(1, z_f[1:(length(y_n_h) - 1 - (h - 1)), 1:cc])           c_hat <- lm.fit(x = reg, y = dep)$coefficients           fc    <- sum(c(1, z_f[T_n, 1:cc]) * c_hat)          }          if (jj == 1) FC_PCA[n, cc]  <- fc         if (jj == 2) FC_sPCA[n, cc] <- fc        }      }    }    # R²_OS for each number of factors   r2_pca <- r2_spca <- numeric(nfac_max)   sse_ar <- sum((actual_y - FC_AR)^2)    for (cc in seq_len(nfac_max)) {      r2_pca[cc]  <- 100 * (1 - sum((actual_y - FC_PCA[, cc])^2)  / sse_ar)     r2_spca[cc] <- 100 * (1 - sum((actual_y - FC_sPCA[, cc])^2) / sse_ar)    }    data.frame(K = seq_len(nfac_max), PCA = round(r2_pca, 2), sPCA = round(r2_spca, 2))    }  # Run res <- run_oos(huang2022_ip, huang2022_macro, h = 1, p_max = 1, nfac_max = 5) print(res)

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: Running code produces: 5 factors, PCA achieves \(R^2_{OS}\) = 7.88% sPCA achieves \(R^2_OS\) = 13.17% — matching paper exactly. sPCA consistently outperforms PCA across factor counts, confirming scaling predictors target-predictive slopes concentrates forecasting-relevant information first factors.

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: Predictive alignment: passing shorter target (T-1 elements) full X (T rows) ensures scaling regression uses predictive relationship \(y_{t+1} ~ X_{,t}\) factors extracted full training window. Winsorization: winsorize = TRUE winsor_probs = c(0, 90) caps extreme scaling coefficients 90th percentile, matching authors’ MATLAB implementation. predict(): projects full training X onto estimated sPCA loadings, correctly applying training-window standardization scaling.

title: Replicating Huang et al. (2022)
text: Huang, D., Jiang, F., Li, K., Tong, G., Zhou, G. (2022). Scaled PCA: New Approach Dimension Reduction. Management Science, 68(3), 1678–1695. DOI: 10.1287/mnsc.2021.4020

title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: Instrumented PCA extracts latent factors panel data asset-specific characteristics serve instruments. model \[r_{,t} = \mathbf{z}_{,t}^\top \mathbf{\Gamma} \mathbf{f}_t + \varepsilon_{,t}\] \(r_{,t}\) return (, investment) asset \(\) time \(t\), \(\mathbf{z}_{,t}\) \(L\)-vector characteristics, \(\mathbf{\Gamma}\) \(L \times K\) matrix characteristic loadings, \(\mathbf{f}_t\) \(K\)-vector latent factors. Estimation alternates solving \(\mathbf{f}_t\) given \(\mathbf{\Gamma}\) updating \(\mathbf{\Gamma}\) given \(\mathbf{f}_t\) (ALS), SVD normalization step ensure \(\mathbf{\Gamma}^\top \mathbf{\Gamma} = \mathbf{}_K\).

title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: Grunfeld dataset ships sdim. dependent variable gross investment (invest) two characteristics market value (value) capital stock (capital). ipca_est() expects \(T \times N\) return matrix \(T \times N \times L\) characteristics array:
code: library(sdim)  data(grunfeld) str(grunfeld) #> 'data.frame':    220 obs. of  5 variables: #>  $ firm   : chr  "American Steel" "American Steel" "American Steel" "American Steel" ... #>  $ year   : int  1935 1936 1937 1938 1939 1940 1941 1942 1943 1944 ... #>  $ invest : num  2.94 5.64 10.23 4.05 3.33 ... #>  $ value  : num  30.3 43.9 107 68.3 84.2 ... #>  $ capital: num  52 52.9 54.5 59.7 61.7 ... firms <- sort(unique(grunfeld$firm)) years <- sort(unique(grunfeld$year)) N  <- length(firms) TT <- length(years)  ret <- matrix(NA_real_, TT, N, dimnames = list(years, firms)) Z   <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(TT, N, 2),              dimnames = list(years, firms, c("value", "capital")))  for (i in seq_along(firms)) {      idx <- grunfeld$firm == firms[i]   ret[, i]  <- grunfeld$invest[idx]   Z[, i, 1] <- grunfeld$value[idx]   Z[, i, 2] <- grunfeld$capital[idx]    }  cat("ret:", nrow(ret), "x", ncol(ret), "\n") #> ret: 20 x 11 cat("Z:  ", paste(dim(Z), collapse = " x "), "\n") #> Z:   20 x 11 x 2

title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: Extract one latent factor: returned object contains characteristic loadings (\(\mathbf{\Gamma}\), stored lambda) estimated factors:

what: Validation against the Python ipca package
title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: Python ipca package implements ALS algorithm using Grunfeld dataset example. n_factors = 1 intercept, Python output : Compare loadings factors (sign-aligned):
code:
py_gamma   <- c(0.99166014, 0.12888046) py_factors <- c(   0.1031968381, 0.0884489515, 0.0838496628, 0.0845069923, 0.0722523449,   0.0995068155, 0.1228840058, 0.1422623752, 0.1197532025, 0.1179724004,   0.1087561863, 0.1357521189, 0.1579348267, 0.1660545375, 0.1484923276,   0.1586634303, 0.1596007400, 0.1759379247, 0.1921695585, 0.2111065868 ) r_gamma <- as.numeric(fit$lambda) r_factors <- as.numeric(fit$factors)  # Sign-align if needed if (cor(r_gamma, py_gamma) < 0) {   r_gamma   <- -r_gamma   r_factors <- -r_factors }  cat("Gamma max |diff|:  ", sprintf("%.2e", max(abs(r_gamma - py_gamma))), "
") #> Gamma max |diff|:   3.58e-09 cat("Factor max |diff|: ", sprintf("%.2e", max(abs(r_factors - py_factors))), "
") #> Factor max |diff|:  4.99e-11 cat("Factor correlation:", sprintf("%.10f", cor(r_factors, py_factors)), "
") #> Factor correlation: 1.0000000000

title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: IPCA can also extract one factor. \(K = 2\) (characteristics contribute factor dimension):
code: fit2 <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 2) summary(fit2) #> Instrumented Principal Components Analysis (IPCA)  #> ----------------------------------------  #> Call: ipca_est(ret = ret, Z = Z, nfac = 2) #>  #> Dimensions #> ----------------------------------------  #>  Observations     20 #>  Characteristics  2 #>  Factors          2 #>  Factor mean      zero #>  #> Eigenvalues #> ----------------------------------------  #>                     F1      F2 #> Eigenvalue      0.0197  0.0073 #> Var. expl. (%) 72.9800 27.0200

title: IPCA with the Grunfeld dataset
text: Grunfeld, Y. (1958). Determinants Corporate Investment. Ph.D. thesis, Department Economics, University Chicago. Kelly, B. T., Pruitt, S., Su, Y. (2019). Characteristics Covariances: Unified Model Risk Return. Journal Financial Economics, 134(3), 501–524. DOI: 10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.05.001

title: Get started with sdim
text: sdim implements five factor extraction methods asset pricing macroeconomic forecasting: estimators return S3 objects print(), summary(), predict() methods.

title: Get started with sdim
code: library(sdim)  set.seed(42) X   <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 20), 200, 20) ret <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 30) / 100, 200, 30)

title: Get started with sdim
text: methods take multivariate target (T × N returns) matrix factor proxies (T × L):
code: fit_pca <- pca_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3) fit_pls <- pls_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3) fit_rra <- rra_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3)  print(fit_rra) #> <sdim_fit [rra]> #>  Observations : 200  #>  Predictors   : 20  #>  Factors      : 3

title: Get started with sdim
text: sPCA takes univariate target scales predictor OLS slope target extracting principal components. length(target) < nrow(X), first length(target) rows used scaling regression rows used factor extraction — supports predictive alignment needed --sample forecasting.
code: y <- rnorm(200)  fit_spca <- spca_est(target = y, X = X, nfac = 3) print(fit_spca) #> <sdim_spca> #>  Observations : 200  #>  Predictors   : 20  #>  Factors      : 3

title: Get started with sdim
text: IPCA extracts latent factors panel data using time-varying characteristics instruments:
code: TT <- 120  K <- 50 n_chars <- 6 ret_panel <- matrix(rnorm(TT * K) / 100, TT, K) Z <- array(rnorm(TT * K * n_chars), dim = c(TT, K, n_chars))  fit_ipca <- ipca_est(ret_panel, Z, nfac = 3) #> Warning in ipca_als_cpp(ret_list, z_list, K = nfac, max_iter = max_iter, : #> ipca_est: ALS did not converge in 100 iterations print(fit_ipca) #> <sdim_fit [ipca]> #>  Observations    : 120  #>  Characteristics : 6  #>  Factors         : 3  #>  Factor mean     : zero

title: Get started with sdim
text: Use predict() project new data onto estimated factor loadings:
code: X_new <- matrix(rnorm(5 * 20), 5, 20)  # PCA projection F_new <- predict(fit_pca, X_new) dim(F_new) #> [1] 5 3  # sPCA projection (standardizes newdata using training parameters) F_spca_new <- predict(fit_spca, X_new) dim(F_spca_new) #> [1] 5 3

title: Get started with sdim
text: Evaluate extracted factors using metrics et al. (2023, §2.4):
code: eval_factors(ret = ret, factors = fit_rra$factors) #> Factor Evaluation #> ----------------------------------------  #>  Portfolios       30 #>  Factors          3 #>  #> Performance (He et al., 2023, §2.4) #> ----------------------------------------  #>  RMSPE              0.9875  (%) #>  Total adj-R²       2.9593  (%) #>  SR                 0.0522 #>  A2R                0.9443

title: Get started with sdim
text: package ships datasets replication: grunfeld: Grunfeld (1958) investment panel (11 firms, 20 years) — used IPCA validation. he2023_*: Seven datasets et al. (2023) — factor proxies portfolio returns. huang2022_macro: 720 × 123 matrix transformed FRED-MD predictors Huang et al. (2022). huang2022_ip: IP growth target Huang et al. (2022) --sample exercise. See vignette("ipca-grunfeld"), vignette("he2023-table3"), vignette("huang2022-table4") full examples.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gabriel Cabrera. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cabrera G (2026). sdim: R Package Supervised Dimension Reduction. R package version 0.1.0, https://gabbocg.github.io/sdim/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {sdim: An R Package for Supervised Dimension Reduction},   author = {Gabriel Cabrera},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://gabbocg.github.io/sdim/}, }

title: An R Package for Supervised Dimension Reduction
text: sdim implements five factor extraction methods asset pricing macroeconomic forecasting: PCA, PLS, RRA take multivariate target (T×N returns matrix) matrix factor proxies. sPCA takes univariate target scales proxy OLS slope target extracting principal components. IPCA extracts latent factors panel data using time-varying characteristics instruments, estimated via alternating least squares (ALS). Performance extracted factors can evaluated eval_factors(). package ships seven he2023_* datasets (factor proxies portfolio returns) et al. (2023, MS) replication package.

title: An R Package for Supervised Dimension Reduction
code: # Install from GitHub (not yet on CRAN) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("GabboCg/sdim")

title: An R Package for Supervised Dimension Reduction
code: library(sdim)  set.seed(42) X   <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 20), 200, 20) # T x L factor proxies ret <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 30) / 100, 200, 30) # T x N returns (target)  # Fit each method fit_pca <- pca_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3) fit_pls <- pls_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3) fit_rra <- rra_est(target = ret, X = X, nfac = 3)  print(fit_rra) #> <sdim_fit [rra]> #>  Observations : 200 #>  Predictors   : 20 #>  Factors      : 3  # Evaluate factor quality (RMSPE and total adj-R² from He et al. 2023, §2.4) eval_factors(ret = ret, factors = fit_rra$factors) #> Factor Evaluation #> ---------------------------------------- #>  Portfolios       30 #>  Factors           3 #> #> Performance (He et al., 2023, §2.4) #> ---------------------------------------- #>  RMSPE              0.9875  (%) #>  Total adj-R²       2.9593  (%) #>  SR                 0.0522 #>  A2R                0.9443

title: An R Package for Supervised Dimension Reduction
code: # Simulate panel set.seed(99) TT <- 120 K  <- 50 n_chars <- 6  ret <- matrix(rnorm(TT * K) / 100, TT, K) Z   <- array(rnorm(TT * K * n_chars), dim = c(TT, K, n_chars))  fit_ipca <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 3) print(fit_ipca) #> <sdim_fit [ipca]> #>  Observations    : 120 #>  Characteristics : 6 #>  Factors         : 3 #>  Factor mean     : zero  summary(fit_ipca) #> Instrumented Principal Components Analysis (IPCA) #> ---------------------------------------- #> Call: ipca_est(ret = ret, Z = Z, nfac = 3) #> #> Dimensions #> ---------------------------------------- #>  Observations     120 #>  Characteristics  6 #>  Factors          3 #>  Factor mean      zero #> #> Eigenvalues #> ---------------------------------------- #>                     F1      F2      F3 #> Eigenvalue      0.8952  0.9336  0.8652 #> Var. expl. (%) 33.2300 34.6600 32.1200  # With factor mean specifications fit_const <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 3, factor_mean = "constant") fit_const$mu   # time-series mean of each factor  fit_var <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 3, factor_mean = "VAR1") fit_var$var_coef   # K x K VAR(1) coefficient matrix

title: Estimate AR(p) model — estimate_ar_res
text: Fits autoregressive model order p horizon-h target returns OLS coefficients residuals.

title: Estimate AR(p) model — estimate_ar_res
Estimate AR(p) model — estimate_ar_res
text: y Numeric vector target variable. h Positive integer; forecast horizon. p Non-negative integer; AR lag order.

title: Estimate AR(p) model — estimate_ar_res
text: list components: a_hat Coefficient vector (intercept first). res Residual vector.

title: Estimate AR(p) model — estimate_ar_res
code: y <- arima.sim(list(ar = 0.7), n = 200) ar_fit <- estimate_ar_res(y, h = 1, p = 1) ar_fit$a_hat #>           [,1] #> [1,] 0.1027578 #> [2,] 0.7011676

title: Estimate ARDL(p1, p2) model — estimate_ardl_multi
text: Fits autoregressive distributed lag model horizon-h target, p1 lags y p2 lags additional regressors z (e.g., extracted factors).

title: Estimate ARDL(p1, p2) model — estimate_ardl_multi
Estimate ARDL(p1, p2) model — estimate_ardl_multi
text: y Numeric vector target variable. z Numeric matrix additional regressors (e.g., factor estimates). h Positive integer; forecast horizon. p Integer vector length 2: c(p1, p2) p1 number AR lags p2 number z lags.

title: Estimate ARDL(p1, p2) model — estimate_ardl_multi
text: Coefficient vector (intercept, AR lags, z lags).

title: Estimate ARDL(p1, p2) model — estimate_ardl_multi

what: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
title: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
text: Computes two performance measures , Huang, Li, Zhou (2023), Section 2.4: Total adj-\(R^2\) (Equation 19) root-mean-squared pricing error (RMSPE, Equation 20).

title: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
text: ret Numeric matrix data frame (T x N) excess returns target portfolios. factors Numeric matrix (T x K) extracted factors, e.g. fit$factors pca_est, pls_est, rra_est.

title: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
text: named numeric vector four elements: RMSPE Root-mean-squared pricing error (percent). Average     assets per-asset RMSE \(R_{} - \hat\beta_i' f_t\)     (intercept excluded fitted value), Equation 20.     Multiplied 100 ret decimal units. TotalR2 Total adjusted \(R^2\) (percent), Equation 19. SR Mean absolute alpha--residual-volatility ratio (Sharpe     ratio pricing errors). A2R Mean absolute alpha--mean-return ratio.

title: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
text: , J., Huang, J., Li, F., Zhou, G. (2023).   Shrinking Factor Dimension: Reduced-Rank Approach.   Management Science, 69(9).   doi:10.1287/mnsc.2022.4563

title: Evaluate extracted factors against target returns — eval_factors
code: set.seed(1) ret <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 10) / 100, 100, 10) X   <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 8), 100, 8) fit <- pca_est(X = X, nfac = 3) eval_factors(ret = ret, factors = fit$factors) #> Factor Evaluation #> ----------------------------------------  #>  Portfolios       10 #>  Factors          3 #>  #> Performance (He et al., 2023, §2.4) #> ----------------------------------------  #>  RMSPE              1.0123  (%) #>  Total adj-R²       1.4927  (%) #>  SR                 0.0708 #>  A2R               19.0977

title: Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
text: Panel data gross investment 11 US firms 20 years (1935–1954), originally Grunfeld (1958). classic panel dataset used validating IPCA estimator Python ipca package (Kelly, Pruitt, Su, 2019).

title: Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
text: data.frame 220 rows 5 variables: firm Character; firm name (11 unique firms). year Integer; year observation (1935–1954). invest Numeric; gross investment (millions dollars). value Numeric; market value firm (millions dollars). capital Numeric; stock plant equipment (millions dollars).

title: Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
text: Grunfeld, Y. (1958). Determinants Corporate Investment.   Ph.D. thesis, Department Economics, University Chicago.   Loaded statsmodels Python package   (statsmodels.datasets.grunfeld).

title: Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
text: Kelly, B. T., Pruitt, S., Su, Y. (2019).   Characteristics Covariances: Unified Model Risk Return.   Journal Financial Economics, 134(3), 501–524.   doi:10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.05.001

title: Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset — grunfeld
code: head(grunfeld) #>             firm year invest   value capital #> 1 American Steel 1935  2.938  30.284  52.011 #> 2 American Steel 1936  5.643  43.909  52.903 #> 3 American Steel 1937 10.233 107.020  54.499 #> 4 American Steel 1938  4.046  68.306  59.722 #> 5 American Steel 1939  3.326  84.164  61.659 #> 6 American Steel 1940  4.680  69.157  62.243  # Reshape for ipca_est(): T x N matrix and T x N x L array firms <- sort(unique(grunfeld$firm)) years <- sort(unique(grunfeld$year)) N <- length(firms) TT <- length(years)  ret <- matrix(NA, TT, N) Z   <- array(NA, dim = c(TT, N, 2)) for (i in seq_along(firms)) {   idx <- grunfeld$firm == firms[i]   ret[, i]  <- grunfeld$invest[idx]   Z[, i, 1] <- grunfeld$value[idx]   Z[, i, 2] <- grunfeld$capital[idx] }  fit <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 1) print(fit) #> <sdim_fit [ipca]> #>  Observations    : 20  #>  Characteristics : 2  #>  Factors         : 1  #>  Factor mean     : zero

what: Dacheng 202-portfolio value-weighted returns from He, Huang, Li, Zhou (2023) — he2023_dacheng202
title: Dacheng 202-portfolio value-weighted returns from He, Huang, Li, Zhou (2023) — he2023_dacheng202
text: Monthly value-weighted returns 202 portfolios (Dacheng Xiu's replication data) replication package , Huang, Li, Zhou (2023). Used target return matrix (target) RRA, PLS, PCA estimators. Columns named sequentially p001–p202.

title: Dacheng 202-portfolio value-weighted returns from He, Huang, Li, Zhou (2023) — he2023_dacheng202
Dacheng 202-portfolio value-weighted returns from He, Huang, Li, Zhou (2023) — he2023_dacheng202

Covers output income, labour market, consumption, housing, inventories orders, money credit, interest rates, exchange rates, prices.

IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
text: IPCA factor extraction

title: IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
code: ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac, max_iter = 100, tol = 1e-06, factor_mean = "zero")

title: IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
text: ret Numeric matrix (T x N) asset returns. Use NA missing observations (unbalanced panel). Z Numeric array (T x N x L) asset characteristics. NAs must mirror ret exactly. nfac Positive integer; number latent factors K extract. max_iter Maximum ALS iterations (default 100). tol Convergence tolerance Frobenius norm loading change (default 1e-6). factor_mean Character scalar specifying factor mean modelled. One "zero" (default, mean adjustment), "constant" (time-series average), "VAR1" (VAR(1) intercept).

title: IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
text: object class "sdim_fit" fields:   factors (T x K), lambda (L x K characteristic loadings,   .e. Gamma Kelly et al.), eigvals (factor variances),   factor_mean (character scalar), call,   method = "ipca", nfac.   factor_mean = "constant": also mu (length-K mean vector).   factor_mean = "VAR1": also var_coef (K x K),   var_intercept (length-K), var_resid ((T-1) x K).

title: IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
text: Kelly, B. T., Pruitt, S., Su, Y. (2019).   Characteristics Covariances: Unified Model Risk Return.   Journal Financial Economics, 134(3), 501–524.   doi:10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.05.001

title: IPCA factor extraction — ipca_est
code: set.seed(1) ret <- matrix(rnorm(50 * 10) / 100, 50, 10) Z   <- array(rnorm(50 * 10 * 4), dim = c(50, 10, 4)) fit <- ipca_est(ret, Z, nfac = 2) print(fit) #> <sdim_fit [ipca]> #>  Observations    : 50  #>  Characteristics : 4  #>  Factors         : 2  #>  Factor mean     : zero

Standardize columns to zero mean and unit variance — oos_standardize
text: X numeric matrix.

title: Standardize columns to zero mean and unit variance — oos_standardize
code: X <- matrix(rnorm(100), 20, 5) Xs <- oos_standardize(X) round(colMeans(Xs), 10) #> [1] 0 0 0 0 0 round(apply(Xs, 2, sd), 10) #> [1] 1 1 1 1 1

title: PCA factor extraction — pca_est
text: PCA factor extraction

title: PCA factor extraction — pca_est
PCA factor extraction — pca_est
text: target Ignored; accepted API uniformity estimators. X Numeric matrix data frame (T x L) factor proxies. nfac Positive integer; number factors extract. gamma Numeric scalar controlling mean adjustment second-moment matrix. `gamma = -1` (default) gives sample covariance (traditional PCA). `gamma = 10` `gamma = 1` give Lettau-Ludvigson variants et al. (2023).

title: PCA factor extraction — pca_est
text: object class "sdim_fit".

title: PCA factor extraction — pca_est
text: , J., Huang, J., Li, F., Zhou, G. (2023).   Shrinking Factor Dimension: Reduced-Rank Approach.   Management Science, 69(9).   doi:10.1287/mnsc.2022.4563

title: PCA factor extraction — pca_est
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 8), 100, 8) fit <- pca_est(X = X, nfac = 3) print(fit) #> <sdim_fit [pca]> #>  Observations : 100  #>  Predictors   : 8  #>  Factors      : 3

PLS factor extraction (Matlab-faithful NIPALS algorithm) — pls_est
text: target Numeric matrix (T x N) target variables (e.g., asset returns). vector coerced T x 1 matrix. X Numeric matrix data frame (T x L) factor proxies. nfac Positive integer; number PLS components extract.

title: PLS factor extraction (Matlab-faithful NIPALS algorithm) — pls_est
text: object class "sdim_fit".

title: PLS factor extraction (Matlab-faithful NIPALS algorithm) — pls_est
text: , J., Huang, J., Li, F., Zhou, G. (2023).   Shrinking Factor Dimension: Reduced-Rank Approach.   Management Science, 69(9).   doi:10.1287/mnsc.2022.4563

title: PLS factor extraction (Matlab-faithful NIPALS algorithm) — pls_est
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 8), 100, 8) Y <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 5), 100, 5) fit <- pls_est(target = Y, X = X, nfac = 3) print(fit) #> <sdim_fit [pls]> #>  Observations : 100  #>  Predictors   : 8  #>  Factors      : 3

title: Project new data onto estimated factor loadings — predict.sdim_fit
text: Project new data onto estimated factor loadings

title: Project new data onto estimated factor loadings — predict.sdim_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'sdim_fit' predict(object, newdata, ...)

title: Project new data onto estimated factor loadings — predict.sdim_fit
text: object object class "sdim_fit". newdata numeric matrix data frame number columns original predictor matrix. ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Project new data onto estimated factor loadings — predict.sdim_fit
text: numeric matrix projected factors nrow(newdata) rows   ncol(object$factors) columns.

what: Project new data onto estimated sPCA factor loadings — predict.sdim_spca
title: Project new data onto estimated sPCA factor loadings — predict.sdim_spca
text: Standardizes newdata using training column means standard deviations, scales estimated (possibly winsorized) regression slopes, projects onto sPCA loadings.

title: Project new data onto estimated sPCA factor loadings — predict.sdim_spca
code: # S3 method for class 'sdim_spca' predict(object, newdata, ...)

title: Project new data onto estimated sPCA factor loadings — predict.sdim_spca
text: object object class "sdim_spca". newdata numeric matrix data frame number columns original predictor matrix. ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Project new data onto estimated sPCA factor loadings — predict.sdim_spca
text: numeric matrix projected factors nrow(newdata) rows   ncol(object$factors) columns.

title: Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
text: Implements two-step GMM estimator , Huang, Li, Zhou (2023). Factor proxies X rotated maximise explanatory power target return matrix target, using diagonal GMM weighting matrices.

title: Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
text: target Numeric matrix (T x N) target variables (e.g., asset returns). vector coerced T x 1 matrix. X Numeric matrix data frame (T x L) factor proxies. nfac Positive integer; number RRA factors extract. compute_stat Logical; TRUE, compute GMM J-test statistic overidentifying restrictions. Returned NULL FALSE (default) degrees freedom <= 0.

title: Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
text: object class "sdim_fit".

title: Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
text: , J., Huang, J., Li, F., Zhou, G. (2023).   Shrinking Factor Dimension: Reduced-Rank Approach.   Management Science, 69(9).   doi:10.1287/mnsc.2022.4563

title: Reduced-Rank Approach (RRA) factor extraction — rra_est
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 8), 100, 8) Y <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 5), 100, 5) fit <- rra_est(target = Y, X = X, nfac = 3) print(fit) #> <sdim_fit [rra]> #>  Observations : 100  #>  Predictors   : 8  #>  Factors      : 3

what: Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
title: Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
text: Selects lag order autoregressive model horizon-h target \(y_{t,h}\) minimising Schwarz Information Criterion.

title: Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
text: y Numeric vector target variable. h Positive integer; forecast horizon. h = 1 target simply y. p_max Maximum lag order consider. function evaluates p = 0, 1, ..., p_max.

title: Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
text: Integer: selected lag order. value 0 means intercept-  model preferred.

title: Select AR lag order by SIC (BIC) — select_ar_lag_sic
Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
text: Implements scaled principal component analysis (sPCA): predictors first standardized, standardized predictor scaled univariate predictive slope target, finally principal components extracted scaled predictors.

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
code: spca_est(target, X, nfac, winsorize = FALSE, winsor_probs = c(0, 99))

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
text: target numeric vector length T_reg (T_reg <= T). X numeric matrix data frame T rows N columns. length(target) < nrow(X), first length(target) rows standardized X used scaling regression T rows used standardization factor extraction. matches --sample workflow Huang et al. (2022), predictive regression y_{t+1} ~ X_t uses fewer rows full training window. nfac positive integer giving number factors extract. winsorize Logical; TRUE, winsorize absolute slope estimates scaling predictors. winsor_probs Numeric vector length 2 giving winsorization percentiles. Used winsorize = TRUE.

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
text: object class "sdim_spca" components: factors T x nfac matrix extracted sPCA factors. beta numeric vector predictor-specific predictive slopes. beta_scaled numeric vector scaling coefficients actually used. col_means Column means X (used predict). col_sds Column standard deviations X (used predict). Xs standardized predictor matrix. scaleXs scaled standardized predictor matrix. lambda estimated loading matrix. residuals Residual matrix PCA reconstruction step. ve2 Average squared residual row. eigvals Singular values decomposition scaleXs %*% t(scaleXs). call matched function call.

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
text: function follows MATLAB implementation Huang, Jiang, Li, Tong, Zhou (2022).

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
text: Huang, D., Jiang, F., Li, K., Tong, G., Zhou, G. (2022).   Scaled PCA: New Approach Dimension Reduction.   Management Science, 68(3), 1678–1695.   doi:10.1287/mnsc.2021.4020

title: Scaled PCA factor extraction — spca_est
code: set.seed(123) X <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 10), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) y <- rnorm(200)  fit <- spca_est(target = y, X = X, nfac = 3) dim(fit$factors) #> [1] 200   3 head(fit$beta) #> [1]  0.0007544186  0.0307199352  0.0074016978 -0.0217043884  0.0892871951 #> [6]  0.0260282689  # Predictive alignment: target has fewer rows than X fit2 <- spca_est(target = y[1:199], X = X, nfac = 3) dim(fit2$factors)  # 200 x 3 (factors for all T rows) #> [1] 200   3
